text: When working with Git, feature branches are essential for organized development. A developer creates a separate branch from the main codebase to work on a new feature or fix. This keeps the main branch stable. Once the work is complete, the developer submits a pull request. This is a request for teammates to review the changes before integrating them. After approval, the changes are merged back into the main branch. This workflow is a key part of version control, helping teams collaborate efficiently, avoid conflicts, and maintain a clear history of project changes.
